# Telicent DS Component Manifest

> `@telicent-oss/ds` is Telicent's MUI-based React component library. This file is the complete, self-contained reference: setup, theming, every exported component, and copy-paste recipes. An agent that fetches it has what it needs to build a Telicent app UI without reading the source.

**AGENT INSTRUCTION:** This is your source of truth for UI components. Before writing any custom JSX or Tailwind classes for a visual element, you MUST find the closest match in this document.
- All components are imported directly from `@telicent-oss/ds`. (e.g., `import { Button, Card } from "@telicent-oss/ds";`)
- **Do not** write raw HTML (`<button>`, `<div>` for cards, `<input>`) if a component exists here.
- **Do not** use Tailwind for colors, borders, or shadows on these components; the theme controls them. Set colour through the theme palette (see Setup & Theming). Use Tailwind primarily for spacing (`gap-X`, `mt-X`) and sizing.

> Verified against `@telicent-oss/ds` source (`src/export.ts`, `src/components/*`, `src/theme/*`). Every component named below is a real package export; setup, prop, and theming details are taken from the source and from how Telicent apps (telicent-search, telicent-graph, catalog) wire the library.

---

## Setup & Theming

Install:
```sh
npm install @telicent-oss/ds
```

Peer dependencies the consuming app must also install: `react@^19`, `react-dom@^19`, `@tanstack/react-query`, `@telicent-oss/ontologyservice@^1`, `axios`.

Import the styles once at the app entry. FontAwesome icons render only if the second file is loaded:
```ts
import "@telicent-oss/ds/dist/style.css";
import "@telicent-oss/ds/dist/fontawesome.css";
```

Wrap the app in `UIThemeProvider`. It supplies the MUI theme and `CssBaseline` internally — do not add your own `ThemeProvider` or `CssBaseline`.
```tsx
import { UIThemeProvider } from "@telicent-oss/ds";

<UIThemeProvider theme="DataNavy" dark={false}>
  <App />
</UIThemeProvider>
```
- `theme` (required): one of `"DataNavy"`, `"DocumentPink"`, `"GraphOrange"`, `"AdminBlue"`, `"Blank"`.
- `dark` (optional, default `false`): set it for the dark palette. Dark mode is this prop only — there is no runtime toggle or CSS class.

Read theme colours and tokens in app code with the `useExtendedTheme` hook. This is the sanctioned way to set a colour — use it instead of a Tailwind colour class:
```tsx
import { useExtendedTheme } from "@telicent-oss/ds";

const theme = useExtendedTheme();
// theme.palette.primary.main, theme.palette.tertiary.main, theme.palette.background.default
```
Palette keys include `primary`, `tertiary`, `text`, and `background`.

> Auth (`useAuth`, `AuthProvider`, `AuthContext`, `AuthRedirectUri`, `Callback`, and the `authorize-flow` utilities) is exported from `@telicent-oss/ds` and used by most Telicent apps, but it is out of scope for this UI component reference. See `src/utils/authorize-flow` in the source.

> Geospatial map components (`BasicMap`, `BasicMapV2`, `FeatureMap`, `MapCanvas`, `MapCanvasV2`, `LayerSelector`, `MapCanvasProvider`, and the `useMap` hook) are exported from `@telicent-oss/ds` and used by telicent-search and telicent-graph, but they are out of scope for this general UI component reference. See `src/feature-components/Map` in the source.

---

## 1. Buttons & Actions
*Use these instead of standard `<button>` tags. There is one `Button` component; the visual style is a `variant` prop, not a separate component per style.*
* **`Button`**: The standard button. Set emphasis with `variant`:
  * `variant="primary"` — main call-to-action on a page or form.
  * `variant="secondary"` — alternative actions (e.g. "Cancel", "Back").
  * `variant="tertiary"` — low-emphasis, neutral-colour actions.
  * `variant="text"` — text-only clickable actions (no background).
  * `variant="base"` — unstyled button base.
  * Size via `size="small" | "medium" | "large"` (default `medium`).
  * `onClick`, `type`, `disabled`, `startIcon`, `endIcon` pass through to MUI Button. There is **no `loading` prop** — show a pending state with `disabled` plus a label change, or an inline `<Spinner size={16} />` (see Recipes).
```yaml
Button:
  variant: {type: '"base" | "text" | "primary" | "secondary" | "tertiary"', required: false}
```
Live: https://telicent-oss.github.io/telicent-ds/?path=/docs/buttons-button--docs
Source: https://github.com/telicent-oss/telicent-ds/blob/main/src/components/buttons/Button/Button.stories.tsx
Default args: children="Button", onClick=fn(), variant="primary"

Demonstrated states:
- Primary — variant="primary"
- Secondary — variant="secondary"
- Tertiary — variant="tertiary"
- Text — variant="text"
- Base — variant="base"
- Sizes
- StartIcon
- EndIcon
- FullWidth
- SXOverrides
* **`IconButton`**: Use when a button only contains an Icon. Should include an `aria-label`.
* **`DropdownButton`**: Use for buttons that trigger a menu of options. (This is also the menu primitive — there is no standalone `Menu` export.)
```yaml
DropdownButton:
  ariaLabel: {type: 'string', required: true}
  id: {type: 'string', required: true}
  menuItems: {type: 'DropDownButtonMenuItemProps[]', required: true}
```
Live: https://telicent-oss.github.io/telicent-ds/?path=/docs/navigation-menu--docs
Source: https://github.com/telicent-oss/telicent-ds/blob/main/src/components/Navigation/Menu/Menu.stories.tsx

Demonstrated states:
- Default — "aria-label"="menu", menuId="menu-default", options=[…], button=fn
- WithIconsAndDisabled — "aria-label"="menu with icons", menuId="menu-icons", options=[…], button=fn
- LinkNavigation — "aria-label"="navigation menu", menuId="menu-links", options=[…], button=fn
- WithDividerAndOnSelect — "aria-label"="menu with divider", menuId="menu-divider", options=[…], onSelect=fn, button=fn
- WithSelected — "aria-label"="menu with selected item", menuId="menu-selected", options=[…], button=fn
* **`TooltipToggleButton`**: Toggle actions that require hover context.
```yaml
TooltipToggleButton:
  tooltip: {type: 'string', required: false}
```
* **`CopyToClipboard`**: Utility component specifically for copy-to-clipboard actions.
```yaml
CopyToClipboard:
  ariaLabel: {type: 'string', required: false}
  style: {type: 'React.CSSProperties & String', required: false}
  successMsg: {type: 'string', required: false}
  testFailure: {type: 'boolean', required: false}
  text: {type: 'string', required: true}
  variant: {type: '"base" | "text" | "primary" | "secondary" | "tertiary"', required: false}
```
Live: https://telicent-oss.github.io/telicent-ds/?path=/docs/buttons-copytoclipboard--docs
Source: https://github.com/telicent-oss/telicent-ds/blob/main/src/components/buttons/CopyToClipboard/CopyToClipboard.stories.tsx

Demonstrated states:
- Default — text="this is a default example string", ariaLabel="Copy to clipboard button"
- CustomSuccessMessage — text="Hooray!", successMsg="Hooray!!"
- CustomStyle — text="Text for clipboard via WithCustomStyle", ariaLabel="Copy to clipboard button with white color", sx={…}
- Error — testFailure=true, text="This will fail"
* **`Link`**: Use for navigation links. (There is no `LinkButton`; for a link that should read as a link, use `Link`; for a link-styled action, use `Button variant="text"`.)
Live: https://telicent-oss.github.io/telicent-ds/?path=/docs/buttons-link--docs
Source: https://github.com/telicent-oss/telicent-ds/blob/main/src/components/buttons/Link/Link.stories.tsx

Demonstrated states:
- Default
- Disabled

## 2. Layout & Surfaces
*Use these to group content. Avoid writing custom `div` structures with manual padding/borders.*
* **`FlexBox`**: The main layout primitive (wraps MUI `Stack`), used more than any other component. Props: `direction="row" | "column"`, `spacing`, `gap` / `columnGap` / `rowGap`, `justifyContent`, `alignItems`, plus any MUI sx system prop.
* **`FlexGrid` / `FlexGridItem`**: 12-column grid (wrap MUI `Grid`). `FlexGrid` is the container, `FlexGridItem` the child slot; props pass through to MUI Grid (`spacing`, `columns`, breakpoint sizes).
Live: https://telicent-oss.github.io/telicent-ds/?path=/docs/component-library-flexgrid--docs
Source: https://github.com/telicent-oss/telicent-ds/blob/main/src/components/layout/FlexGrid.stories.tsx

Demonstrated states:
- BasicColumns
- Rows
- MainDetailLayout
* **`Container`**: Use to wrap page content and restrict maximum width.
Live: https://telicent-oss.github.io/telicent-ds/?path=/docs/layout-container--docs
Source: https://github.com/telicent-oss/telicent-ds/blob/main/src/components/layout/Container/Container.stories.tsx

Demonstrated states:
- Basic
* **`Brand`**: Telicent brand mark component for use in headers and identity contexts.
```yaml
Brand:
  appName: {type: 'string', required: false}
  beta: {type: 'boolean', required: false}
  size: {type: '"base" | "jumbo"', required: false}
```
Live: https://telicent-oss.github.io/telicent-ds/?path=/docs/brand-brand--docs
Source: https://github.com/telicent-oss/telicent-ds/blob/main/src/components/Brand/Brand.stories.tsx
Default args: appName="Graph", beta=false, size="base"

Demonstrated states:
- Default
- WithBeta — beta=true
- Jumbo — size="jumbo", beta=true
- WithoutAppName
- AsLink — href="https://telicent.io", target="_blank", rel="noreferrer", beta=true
- OnDarkBackground — appName="Admin", beta=true
* **`Card`**: The primary container for distinct data entities.
  * *Composition:* Usually composed with `CardContent` (for data) and `CardActions` (for buttons). A `CardHeader` export also exists (passes through to MUI CardHeader), though most apps omit it and put the title inside `CardContent`.
  * *Props:* `maxWidth` is a DS-specific prop; other props pass through to MUI Card (e.g. `variant="outlined"`).
```yaml
Card:
  maxWidth: {type: 'Property.MaxWidth<string | number>', required: false}
```
```yaml
CardContent:
  sx: {type: 'SxProps<Theme>', required: false}
```
Live: https://telicent-oss.github.io/telicent-ds/?path=/docs/surfaces-card--docs
Source: https://github.com/telicent-oss/telicent-ds/blob/main/src/components/surfaces/Card/Card.stories.tsx

Demonstrated states:
- OutlinedCard — variant="outlined"
* **`Paper`**: A basic elevated surface background without the strict structure of a Card.
Live: https://telicent-oss.github.io/telicent-ds/?path=/docs/surfaces-paper--docs
Source: https://github.com/telicent-oss/telicent-ds/blob/main/src/components/surfaces/Paper/Paper.stories.tsx

Demonstrated states:
- Demo
* **`PopOver`**: Use for tooltip-style overlays and contextual pop-up content.
```yaml
PopOver:
  anchorEl: {type: 'Element | (() => Element) | PopoverVirtualElement | (() => PopoverVirtualElement)', required: false}
  anchorOrigin: {type: 'PopoverOrigin', required: false}
  anchorPosition: {type: 'PopoverPosition', required: false}
  anchorReference: {type: 'PopoverReference', required: false}
  children: {type: 'React.ReactNode', required: false}
  hideCloseButton: {type: 'boolean', required: false}
  onClose: {type: '((event: {}, reason: "backdropClick" | "escapeKeyDown" | "closeButtonClick") => void)', required: true}
  open: {type: 'boolean', required: true}
  transformOrigin: {type: 'PopoverOrigin', required: false}
  width: {type: 'number', required: false}
```
Live: https://telicent-oss.github.io/telicent-ds/?path=/docs/surfaces-popover--docs
Source: https://github.com/telicent-oss/telicent-ds/blob/main/src/components/surfaces/PopOver/PopOver.stories.tsx
Default args: onClose=fn()

Demonstrated states:
- Demo — open=false
* **`FixedPanel` / `FloatingPanel`**: Use for sidebars, property panels, or tool windows.
  * `FloatingPanel` exposes three sub-components: `FloatingPanel.DraggablePanel`, `FloatingPanel.Provider`, and `FloatingPanel.ToggleButton`.
  * `DraggableFloatingPanel` is the same component as `FloatingPanel.DraggablePanel`, exported directly. Use the named export for a standalone draggable panel; use the `FloatingPanel.*` namespace when you also need `.Provider` / `.ToggleButton` (also exported flat as `FloatingPanelProvider` and `FloatingPanelToggleButton`).
```yaml
DraggableFloatingPanel:
  bounds: {type: 'string | Element', required: false}
  children: {type: 'React.ReactNode', required: false}
  count: {type: 'number', required: false}
  defaultPosition: {type: '{ x: number; y: number; }', required: false}
  dragHandleClassName: {type: 'string', required: false}
  iconBeforeTitle: {type: 'React.ReactNode', required: false}
  menu: {type: 'any', required: false}
  targetId: {type: 'string', required: true}
  title: {type: 'string', required: true}
```
Live: https://telicent-oss.github.io/telicent-ds/?path=/docs/surfaces-fixedpanel--docs
Source: https://github.com/telicent-oss/telicent-ds/blob/main/src/components/surfaces/FixedPanel/FixedPanel.stories.tsx

Demonstrated states:
- Demo — count=3, iconBeforeTitle=<jsx>, title="Nodes with geo data", targetId="map"
Live: https://telicent-oss.github.io/telicent-ds/?path=/docs/surfaces-floatingpanel-floatingpanel-draggablepanel--docs
Source: https://github.com/telicent-oss/telicent-ds/blob/main/src/components/surfaces/FloatingPanel/DraggableFloatingPanel.stories.tsx

Demonstrated states:
- ResizeBaseline — bounds="parent", title="Resize baseline", targetId="map", iconBeforeTitle=<jsx>
* **`Drawer`**: Collapsible side panel with a built-in toggle tab. `children` is the panel content; `drawerWidth` sets its open width; `initialOpen` sets the starting state; `onVisibilityChange(open)` and `onToggle` report toggles. Style the surface with `PaperProps` / `PaperSx`.
```yaml
Drawer:
  children: {type: 'React.ReactNode', required: true}
  drawerWidth: {type: 'number', required: false}
  id: {type: 'string', required: false}
  initialOpen: {type: 'boolean', required: false}
  onToggle: {type: 'React.MouseEventHandler<HTMLButtonElement>', required: false}
  onVisibilityChange: {type: '(open: boolean) => void', required: false}
  PaperProps: {type: 'Partial<PaperProps<React.ElementType<any, keyof React.JSX.IntrinsicElements>>>', required: false}
  PaperSx: {type: 'SxProps<Theme>', required: false}
```
Live: https://telicent-oss.github.io/telicent-ds/?path=/docs/component-library-drawer--docs
Source: https://github.com/telicent-oss/telicent-ds/blob/main/src/feature-components/Drawer/composites/Drawer.stories.tsx

Demonstrated states:
- Default — children=<jsx>
- CustomWidth — drawerWidth=200, PaperSx={…}
- PresentationalAndHook
- ImperativeApi
* **`AppBar` / `Toolbar` / `AppChrome`**: Use strictly for top-level application navigation and headers. `AppChrome` is the app shell; pair it with `AppSwitch` for cross-app navigation.
```yaml
AppBar:
  appName: {type: 'string', required: false}
  beta: {type: 'boolean', required: false}
  disableBrand: {type: 'boolean', required: false}
  endChild: {type: 'React.ReactNode', required: false}
  href: {type: 'string', required: false}
  isElevated: {type: 'boolean', required: false}
  position: {type: '"fixed" | "absolute" | "sticky" | "static" | "relative"', required: false}
  startChild: {type: 'React.ReactNode', required: false}
  target: {type: 'React.HTMLAttributeAnchorTarget', required: false}
  version: {type: 'string', required: false}
```
Live: https://telicent-oss.github.io/telicent-ds/?path=/docs/surfaces-appbar--docs
Source: https://github.com/telicent-oss/telicent-ds/blob/main/src/components/surfaces/AppBar/AppBar.stories.tsx

Demonstrated states:
- Default
- WithSignOutButton — appName="Catalogue"
- WithNoBrand — disableBrand=true, startChild=<jsx>, endChild=UserProfileExample
- ClickableBrand — appName="Catalogue", href="https://telicent.io", target="_blank"
- UsageExample — appName="Catalogue", isElevated=true, startChild=<jsx>, endChild=UserProfileExample
Live: https://telicent-oss.github.io/telicent-ds/?path=/docs/surfaces-toolbar--docs
Source: https://github.com/telicent-oss/telicent-ds/blob/main/src/components/surfaces/Toolbar/Toolbar.stories.tsx

Demonstrated states:
- Example

## 3. Inputs & Forms
*Use these instead of standard `<input>`, `<select>`, or `<textarea>` tags. Ensure accessibility labels are passed.*
* **`TextField`**: Standard text input (wraps MUI TextField). Set the caption with `label`; use `value`/`onChange` as usual; DS adds `errorText` for an inline error message. Other props pass through to MUI TextField.
Live: https://telicent-oss.github.io/telicent-ds/?path=/docs/inputs-textfield--docs
Source: https://github.com/telicent-oss/telicent-ds/blob/main/src/components/inputs/TextField/TextField.stories.tsx

Demonstrated states:
- Default — label="Full Name", placeholder="Enter your name", error=false, errorText="This field is required"
- MultiLine — label="Multiline Input", placeholder="type anything that needs a lot of space in here", error=false, multiline=true, rows=10
- HelperText
- ErrorMessage — label="Title", error=true, errorText="Something went wrong, please try again."
* **`EditableTextField`**: Use for inline-editing scenarios (e.g., clicking a title to rename it).
Live: https://telicent-oss.github.io/telicent-ds/?path=/docs/inputs-editable-textfield--docs
Source: https://github.com/telicent-oss/telicent-ds/blob/main/src/components/inputs/EditableTextField/EditableTextField.stories.tsx

Demonstrated states:
- Basic
- FullWidth
- CustomWidth
- WithErrorText
- NoInitialValue
* **`SearchBox` / `MiniSearchBox`**: Use specifically for search inputs.
```yaml
SearchBox:
  autoFocus: {type: 'boolean', required: false}
  inputRef: {type: 'React.ForwardedRef<HTMLInputElement>', required: false}
  name: {type: 'string', required: false}
  onBlur: {type: '(event: React.FocusEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => void', required: false}
  onChange: {type: '(event: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => void', required: false}
  onSearch: {type: '(event: React.SubmitEvent<HTMLFormElement>) => void', required: false}
  placeholder: {type: 'string', required: false}
  sx: {type: 'SxProps', required: false}
  value: {type: 'Value', required: false}
  width: {type: 'number', required: false}
```
```yaml
MiniSearchBox:
  autoFocus: {type: 'boolean', required: false}
  disabled: {type: 'boolean', required: false}
  endIcon: {type: 'React.ReactNode', required: false}
  error: {type: 'boolean', required: false}
  fullWidth: {type: 'boolean', required: false}
  helperText: {type: 'string', required: false}
  id: {type: 'string', required: false}
  inputRef: {type: 'React.Ref<any>', required: false}
  loading: {type: 'boolean', required: false}
  maxRows: {type: 'string | number', required: false}
  minRows: {type: 'string | number', required: false}
  multiline: {type: 'boolean', required: false}
  name: {type: 'string', required: false}
  onBlur: {type: 'React.FocusEventHandler<HTMLInputElement | HTMLTextAreaElement>', required: false}
  onChange: {type: 'React.ChangeEventHandler<HTMLInputElement | HTMLTextAreaElement, Element>', required: false}
  onFocus: {type: 'React.FocusEventHandler<HTMLInputElement | HTMLTextAreaElement>', required: false}
  onInvalid: {type: 'React.FormEventHandler<HTMLInputElement | HTMLTextAreaElement>', required: false}
  onKeyDown: {type: 'React.KeyboardEventHandler<HTMLInputElement | HTMLTextAreaElement>', required: false}
  onKeyUp: {type: 'React.KeyboardEventHandler<HTMLInputElement | HTMLTextAreaElement>', required: false}
  onSearch: {type: 'React.MouseEventHandler<HTMLButtonElement>', required: false}
  onTogglePopOver: {type: 'React.MouseEventHandler<HTMLButtonElement>', required: false}
  placeholder: {type: 'string', required: false}
  progressProps: {type: 'ProgressProps', required: false}
  rows: {type: 'string | number', required: false}
  value: {type: 'unknown', required: false}
```
Live: https://telicent-oss.github.io/telicent-ds/?path=/docs/inputs-search-minisearchbox--docs
Source: https://github.com/telicent-oss/telicent-ds/blob/main/src/components/inputs/SearchBox/MiniSearchBox.stories.tsx
Default args: onSearch=fn(), onTogglePopOver=fn()

Demonstrated states:
- Demo — placeholder="Search"
- WithDownArrow — placeholder="Search"
- Loading — placeholder="Loading", loading=true
* **`Autocomplete` / `MiniSearchAutocomplete`**: Use when search requires type-ahead dropdown suggestions.
```yaml
MiniSearchAutocomplete:
  disabled: {type: 'boolean', required: false}
  endIcon: {type: 'React.ReactNode', required: false}
  error: {type: 'boolean', required: false}
  id: {type: 'string', required: false}
  loading: {type: 'boolean', required: false}
  name: {type: 'string', required: false}
  onKeyDown: {type: 'React.KeyboardEventHandler<HTMLInputElement>', required: false}
  onSearch: {type: 'React.MouseEventHandler<HTMLElement>', required: false}
  placeholder: {type: 'string', required: false}
  progressProps: {type: 'ProgressProps', required: false}
  renderOption: {type: '(props: React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLLIElement> & { key: any; }, option: Value) => React.ReactNode', required: false}
```
Live: https://telicent-oss.github.io/telicent-ds/?path=/docs/inputs-autocomplete--docs
Source: https://github.com/telicent-oss/telicent-ds/blob/main/src/components/inputs/Autocomplete/Autocomplete.stories.tsx

Demonstrated states:
- Basic
- Preselected
- WithHelperText
- Disabled
- AsyncLoading
- StoresIdInFormState
- MultiSelectWithLimit
Live: https://telicent-oss.github.io/telicent-ds/?path=/docs/inputs-search-minisearchautocomplete--docs
Source: https://github.com/telicent-oss/telicent-ds/blob/main/src/components/inputs/SearchBox/MiniSearchAutocomplete.stories.tsx

Demonstrated states:
- RecentSearches — options=[…], placeholder="Search"
- SearchResults — options=[…]
* **`SearchAutocompleteDialog`**: Full-screen dialog combining search input with autocomplete. Composes with `RecentSearches` (renders recent search history) and `SearchResults` (renders the results list).
```yaml
SearchAutocompleteDialog:
  autoFocus: {type: 'boolean', required: false}
  autoHighlight: {type: 'boolean', required: false}
  children: {type: 'React.ReactNode', required: true}
  error: {type: 'boolean', required: false}
  getOptionKey: {type: '(option: string | SearchOption) => string | number', required: false}
  getOptionLabel: {type: '(option: string | SearchOption) => string', required: false}
  helperText: {type: 'string', required: false}
  id: {type: 'string', required: false}
  inputValue: {type: 'string', required: false}
  isOptionEqualToValue: {type: '(option: SearchOption, value: SearchOption) => boolean', required: false}
  label: {type: 'string', required: false}
  loading: {type: 'boolean', required: false}
  loadingText: {type: 'React.ReactNode', required: false}
  name: {type: 'string', required: false}
  onChange: {type: '(event: React.SyntheticEvent, value: string | SearchOption, reason: AutocompleteChangeReason, details?: ...', required: false}
  onClose: {type: '(event: {}, reason: "backdropClick" | "escapeKeyDown") => void', required: false}
  onInputChange: {type: '(event: React.SyntheticEvent, value: string, reason: AutocompleteInputChangeReason) => void', required: false}
  open: {type: 'boolean', required: true}
  openOnFocus: {type: 'boolean', required: false}
  options: {type: 'readonly SearchOption[]', required: true}
  placeholder: {type: 'string', required: false}
  width: {type: 'number', required: false}
```
* **`Select`**: Standard dropdown (wraps MUI Select). Supply choices via the `options` prop — an array of `{ value, label }`, **not** `<MenuItem>` children. Use `value`/`onChange`/`label` as usual; optional `width`, `helperText`.
```yaml
Select:
  helperText: {type: 'React.ReactNode', required: false}
  options: {type: 'Options[]', required: true}
  width: {type: 'string | number', required: false}
```
Live: https://telicent-oss.github.io/telicent-ds/?path=/docs/inputs-select--docs
Source: https://github.com/telicent-oss/telicent-ds/blob/main/src/components/inputs/Select/Select.stories.tsx

Demonstrated states:
- Default — label="Select an option", value="option1", id="select-disabled", options=OPTIONS, width=300, onChange=fn
- Disabled — label="Select an option", value="none", id="select-disabled", options=OPTIONS, width=300, onChange=fn, disabled=true
- WithCustomWidth — label="Select an option", value="option2", id="select-disabled", options=OPTIONS, width=500, onChange=fn
- WithNoLabel — value="option2", id="select-disabled", options=OPTIONS, onChange=fn
- ExampleWithOnChange — width=250, disabled=false
* **`Checkbox`**: Boolean or multi-select input (wraps MUI Checkbox). `label` renders the caption; `checked`/`onChange` control state.
```yaml
Checkbox:
  label: {type: 'string', required: false}
  required: {type: 'boolean', required: false}
```
Live: https://telicent-oss.github.io/telicent-ds/?path=/docs/inputs-checkbox--docs
Source: https://github.com/telicent-oss/telicent-ds/blob/main/src/components/inputs/Checkbox/Checkbox.stories.tsx

Demonstrated states:
- Default — checked=true, id="checkbox-default", onChange=fn
- Disabled — checked=false, id="checkbox-disabled", disabled=true, onChange=fn
- WithCustomLabel — checked=true, id="checkbox-custom-label", label="Custom Label", onChange=fn
- WithRequired — checked=false, id="checkbox-custom-label", label="Required", required=true, onChange=fn
- WithNoLabel — checked=true, id="checkbox-custom-label", required=true, onChange=fn
- ExampleWithOnChange — disabled=false
* **`Switch`**: On/Off toggle (wraps MUI Switch). `label`, `labelPlacement="end" | "start" | "top" | "bottom"` (default `end`), `checked`/`onChange`.
Live: https://telicent-oss.github.io/telicent-ds/?path=/docs/inputs-switch--docs
Source: https://github.com/telicent-oss/telicent-ds/blob/main/src/components/inputs/Switch/Switch.stories.tsx

Demonstrated states:
- Default — checked=true, id="switch-default", onChange=fn
- Disabled — checked=false, id="switch-disabled", disabled=true, onChange=fn
- WithLabel — checked=true, id="switch-with-label", label="Enable feature", onChange=fn
- Required — checked=false, id="switch-required", label="Required setting", required=true, onChange=fn
- NoLabel — checked=true, id="switch-no-label", onChange=fn
- ExampleWithOnChange — disabled=false
* **`DatePicker` / `DateTimePicker`**: Use for any temporal inputs.
```yaml
DatePicker:
  error: {type: 'boolean', required: false}
  errorMsg: {type: 'String', required: false}
  fullWidth: {type: 'boolean', required: false}
  helperText: {type: 'string', required: false}
  label: {type: 'ReactNode & string', required: false}
  maxDate: {type: 'PickerValidDate', required: false}
  minDate: {type: 'PickerValidDate', required: false}
  textFieldProps: {type: 'TextFieldProps', required: false}
  value: {type: 'Dayjs | null', required: true}
```
```yaml
DateTimePicker:
  error: {type: 'boolean', required: false}
  errorMsg: {type: 'string', required: false}
  fullWidth: {type: 'boolean', required: false}
  helperText: {type: 'string', required: false}
  maxDateTime: {type: 'PickerValidDate', required: false}
  minDateTime: {type: 'PickerValidDate', required: false}
  textFieldProps: {type: 'TextFieldProps', required: false}
```
Live: https://telicent-oss.github.io/telicent-ds/?path=/docs/inputs-datepicker--docs
Source: https://github.com/telicent-oss/telicent-ds/blob/main/src/components/inputs/DatePicker/DatePicker.stories.tsx

Demonstrated states:
- Default — label="Pick a date"
- ErrorState — label="Required", errorMsg="Oops, something went wrong with the validation", helperText="Please select a valid date"
- WithDateRestrictions — label="Restricted range", minDate=dayjs().subtract(), maxDate=dayjs().add(), helperText="You can only select dates within ±5 days"
Live: https://telicent-oss.github.io/telicent-ds/?path=/docs/inputs-datetimepicker--docs
Source: https://github.com/telicent-oss/telicent-ds/blob/main/src/components/inputs/DateAndTimePicker/DayTimePicker.stories.tsx

Demonstrated states:
- Default — label="Pick date & time"
- ErrorState — label="Required field", error=true, errorMsg="Something went wrong", helperText="Date and time are required"
- WithDateRestrictions — label="Restricted time range", minDateTime=dayjs().startOf(), maxDateTime=dayjs().add(2, "day").endOf(), helperText="Only available within the next 48 hours"

## 4. Data Display & Typography
*Use these to render text and structured data.*
* **`Text`**: Use for all body typography instead of `<p>` or `<span>`. Style via `variant`: `body1` (default), `body2`, `subtitle1`, `subtitle2`, `caption`, `overline`, `h1`, `h2`, `h3`. (`h4`–`h6` are not valid `Text` variants — use the `H4`–`H6` components for those.)
```yaml
Text:
  paragraph: {type: 'boolean', required: false}
  variant: {type: '"caption" | "h1" | "h2" | "h3" | "overline" | "body1" | "body2" | "subtitle1" | "subtitle2"', required: false}
```
* **`H1` / `H2` / `H3` / `H4` / `H5` / `H6`**: Use for headings instead of `<h1>`–`<h6>`; each renders the matching semantic element. (There is no `Heading` component. `<Text variant="h2">` styles text as a heading but renders a `<p>` — use `H1`–`H6` when the semantic element matters.)
Live: https://telicent-oss.github.io/telicent-ds/?path=/docs/data-display-headings--docs
Source: https://github.com/telicent-oss/telicent-ds/blob/main/src/components/data-display/Text/Heading.stories.tsx
Default args: align="inherit"

Demonstrated states:
- Heading1 — children="Heading Style 1"
- Heading2 — variant="h2", children="Heading Style 2"
- Heading3 — variant="h3", children="Heading Style 3"
- Heading4 — variant="h4", children="Heading Style 4"
- Heading5 — variant="h5", children="Heading Style 5"
- Heading6 — variant="h6", children="Heading Style 6"
* **`TitleAndContent`**: Use for standard "Label: Value" data pairings.
```yaml
TitleAndContent:
  content: {type: 'string', required: false}
  title: {type: 'string', required: false}
```
* **`List`**: Standard list rendering.
* **`TreeView`**: Use for hierarchical or nested data structures.
```yaml
TreeView:
  alwaysExpandedIds: {type: 'string[]', required: false}
  defaultExpandedItems: {type: 'string[]', required: false}
  defaultSelectedItems: {type: 'string | string[]', required: false}
  expandedItems: {type: 'string[]', required: false}
  getItemId: {type: '(item: Item) => string', required: false}
  getItemLabel: {type: '(item: Item) => string', required: false}
  id: {type: 'string', required: false}
  isItemDisabled: {type: '(item: Item) => boolean', required: false}
  items: {type: 'Item[]', required: true}
  multiSelect: {type: 'boolean', required: false}
  onExpandedItemsChange: {type: '(event: React.SyntheticEvent, itemIds: string[]) => void', required: false}
  onItemExpansionToggle: {type: '(event: React.SyntheticEvent, itemId: string, isExpanded: boolean) => void', required: false}
  onItemFocus: {type: '(event: React.SyntheticEvent | null, itemId: string) => void', required: false}
  onItemSelectionToggle: {type: '(event: React.SyntheticEvent, itemId: string, isSelected: boolean) => void', required: false}
  onSelectedItemsChange: {type: '(event: React.SyntheticEvent, itemIds: string[] | string | null) => void', required: false}
  selectedItems: {type: 'string | string[]', required: false}
  sx: {type: 'SxProps', required: false}
```
* **`Chip`**: Use for tags, statuses, or small attributes.
Live: https://telicent-oss.github.io/telicent-ds/?path=/docs/data-display-chip--docs
Source: https://github.com/telicent-oss/telicent-ds/blob/main/src/components/data-display/Chip/Chip.stories.tsx
Default args: label="Chip"

Demonstrated states:
- Filled
- Outlined — variant="outlined"
- ClickableChips
- DeletableChips
* **`Divider`**: Use to separate content sections instead of `<hr>`.
Live: https://telicent-oss.github.io/telicent-ds/?path=/docs/data-display-divider--docs
Source: https://github.com/telicent-oss/telicent-ds/blob/main/src/components/data-display/Divider/Divider.stories.tsx

Demonstrated states:
- Example
- Orientation — orientation="vertical", flexItem=true
- FlexItem
* **`UserProfile` / `UserProfileContent` / `UserStatus`**: Components for rendering user avatars, profile details, and active states. `UserProfileContent` renders the expanded profile detail; `UserStatus` renders the online/status indicator.
```yaml
UserProfile:
  fullName: {type: 'string', required: true}
```
```yaml
UserStatus:
  fullName: {type: 'string', required: true}
```
Live: https://telicent-oss.github.io/telicent-ds/?path=/docs/data-display-user-profile--docs
Source: https://github.com/telicent-oss/telicent-ds/blob/main/src/components/data-display/UserProfile/UserProfile.stories.tsx

Demonstrated states:
- Basic — fullName="Satoru Gojo"
* **`AppSwitch`**: Specific component for switching between different applications.
Live: https://telicent-oss.github.io/telicent-ds/?path=/docs/data-display-app-switch--docs
Source: https://github.com/telicent-oss/telicent-ds/blob/main/src/components/data-display/AppSwitch/AppSwitch.stories.tsx
Default args: apps=appList

Demonstrated states:
- AppSwitchExample — apps=appList
* **`IESType`**: Specific component for rendering IES data types.
```yaml
IESType:
  backgroundColor: {type: 'string', required: true}
  borderColor: {type: 'string', required: true}
  color: {type: 'string', required: true}
  iconClass: {type: 'string', required: true}
```

## 5. Feedback & States
*Use these to communicate system status to the user.*
* **`Spinner`**: Circular loading indicator (wraps MUI CircularProgress; props `size`, `color`). Use for localized loading — inside a button or a small card.
Live: https://telicent-oss.github.io/telicent-ds/?path=/docs/feedback-spinner--docs
Source: https://github.com/telicent-oss/telicent-ds/blob/main/src/components/feedback/Spinner/Spinner.stories.tsx
Default args: size=40, color="primary"

Demonstrated states:
- Default
- Small — size=24
- Large — size=64
- Determinate — variant="determinate", value=65
- InContainer
* **`LinearProgress`**: Horizontal loading bar for page-level or wide-component loading.
Live: https://telicent-oss.github.io/telicent-ds/?path=/docs/feedback-linear-progress--docs
Source: https://github.com/telicent-oss/telicent-ds/blob/main/src/components/feedback/LinearProgress/LinearProgress.stories.tsx

Demonstrated states:
- Basic
* **`Skeleton`**: Use to render placeholder UI while `useQuery` is in an `isLoading` state.
Live: https://telicent-oss.github.io/telicent-ds/?path=/docs/feedback-skeleton--docs
Source: https://github.com/telicent-oss/telicent-ds/blob/main/src/components/feedback/Skeleton/Skeleton.stories.tsx

Demonstrated states:
- Basic
* **`Dialog`**: Interrupt the user for confirmation or sub-forms (wraps MUI Dialog). Required `open` and `onClose`; compose with `DialogTitle` / `DialogContent` / `DialogActions`.
```yaml
Dialog:
  children: {type: 'React.ReactNode', required: true}
  onClose: {type: '() => void', required: true}
  open: {type: 'boolean', required: true}
```
Live: https://telicent-oss.github.io/telicent-ds/?path=/docs/feedback-dialog--docs
Source: https://github.com/telicent-oss/telicent-ds/blob/main/src/components/feedback/Dialog/Dialog.stories.tsx

Demonstrated states:
- Basic
* **`Modal`**: Lower-level overlay with its own close button (`onClose(event, reason)`, optional `hideCloseButton`).
```yaml
Modal:
  hideCloseButton: {type: 'boolean', required: false}
  onClose: {type: '((event: {}, reason: "backdropClick" | "escapeKeyDown" | "closeButtonClick") => void)', required: true}
```
Live: https://telicent-oss.github.io/telicent-ds/?path=/docs/utils-modal--docs
Source: https://github.com/telicent-oss/telicent-ds/blob/main/src/components/utils/Modal/Modal.stories.tsx

Demonstrated states:
- Example
- WithNoCloseButton
* **`ErrorFallback`** (and `ErrorFallbackWrapper`, `ErrorFallbackText`): Render as the fallback of a React error boundary to display crash states gracefully. `ErrorFallbackText` is the text-only variant (no surrounding box). (The boundary itself is not a DS export; the DS provides the fallback UI.)
```yaml
ErrorFallback:
  height: {type: 'string | number', required: false}
  message: {type: 'string', required: false}
  name: {type: 'string', required: false}
  sx: {type: 'SxProps<Theme>', required: false}
```
```yaml
ErrorFallbackWrapper:
  children: {type: 'React.ReactNode', required: true}
  height: {type: 'string | number', required: false}
  sx: {type: 'SxProps<Theme>', required: false}
```
```yaml
ErrorFallbackText:
  message: {type: 'string', required: false}
  name: {type: 'string', required: false}
```
Live: https://telicent-oss.github.io/telicent-ds/?path=/docs/utils-errorfallback--docs
Source: https://github.com/telicent-oss/telicent-ds/blob/main/src/components/utils/ErrorFallback/ErrorFallback.stories.tsx
Default args: name="ExampleComponent", height=256

Demonstrated states:
- DefaultError — name="ExampleComponent"
- CustomMessage — name="DataWidget", message="Unable to load user data."
- CustomNameAndStyle — name="ChartComponent", sx={…}
- ExplicitHeight — name="ProfileCard", height=150

## 6. Icons
*The DS includes two icon sets. Import them directly from `@telicent-oss/ds`.*
* **SVG Icons** (custom Telicent icons): `BinIcon`, `CloseIcon`, `DataServiceIcon`, `DataSetIcon`, `DragHandleIcon`, `FloppyDiskIcon`, `GridIcon`, `ListIcon`, `LocationOnIcon`, `MapIcon`, `MinusCircleIcon`, `PlayIcon`, `PlusCircleIcon`, `TelicentMark`, `TelicentHorizontalSVG`, `UserIcon`, `XIcon`.
* **FontAwesome Icons** (wrapped FA icons): `ClockIcon`, `DownArrowIcon`, `DownloadIcon`, `SearchIcon`.
* **`TeliTypeIcon`**: Renders a type indicator icon for IES/ontology types.
Live: https://telicent-oss.github.io/telicent-ds/?path=/docs/data-display-icons--docs
Source: https://github.com/telicent-oss/telicent-ds/blob/main/src/components/data-display/Icons/index.stories.tsx

Demonstrated states:
- AllIcons

---

## Recipes

*Real shapes from Telicent apps. Copy and adapt.*

**Themed app root** — styles, then the provider (which supplies the MUI theme + CssBaseline):
```tsx
import "@telicent-oss/ds/dist/style.css";
import "@telicent-oss/ds/dist/fontawesome.css";
import { UIThemeProvider } from "@telicent-oss/ds";

export function Root() {
  return (
    <UIThemeProvider theme="DataNavy" dark={false}>
      <App />
    </UIThemeProvider>
  );
}
```

**A form** — note `Select` takes an `options` array, not `<MenuItem>` children:
```tsx
import { FlexBox, TextField, Select, Checkbox, Button } from "@telicent-oss/ds";

<FlexBox direction="column" gap={2}>
  <TextField label="Name" value={name} onChange={(e) => setName(e.target.value)} />
  <Select
    label="Colour"
    value={colour}
    onChange={(e) => setColour(e.target.value)}
    options={[{ value: "r", label: "Red" }, { value: "b", label: "Blue" }]}
  />
  <Checkbox label="I agree" checked={agreed} onChange={(e) => setAgreed(e.target.checked)} />
  <Button variant="primary" onClick={submit}>Save</Button>
</FlexBox>
```

**A react-hook-form form** — the common shape in Telicent apps: uncontrolled inputs (`TextField`) take `register`; controlled inputs (`Select`, `Autocomplete`) go through `Controller` and spread `field`:
```tsx
import { useForm, Controller } from "react-hook-form";
import { FlexBox, TextField, Select, Autocomplete, Button } from "@telicent-oss/ds";

const { register, control, handleSubmit, formState: { isValid } } = useForm<FormValues>();

<FlexBox direction="column" gap={2} component="form" onSubmit={handleSubmit(onSave)}>
  <TextField label="Name" {...register("name")} />
  <Controller
    name="classification"
    control={control}
    render={({ field }) => (
      <Select label="Classification" options={CLASSIFICATION_OPTIONS} {...field} fullWidth />
    )}
  />
  <Controller
    name="nationality"
    control={control}
    render={({ field }) => <Autocomplete label="Nationality" options={countryOptions} {...field} />}
  />
  <Button variant="primary" type="submit" disabled={!isValid}>Save</Button>
</FlexBox>
```

**A loading button** — there is no `loading` prop; gate on `disabled` and swap the label:
```tsx
import { Button, Spinner } from "@telicent-oss/ds";

<Button
  variant="primary"
  disabled={isPending}
  startIcon={isPending ? <Spinner size={16} color="inherit" /> : undefined}
>
  {isPending ? "Saving..." : "Save"}
</Button>
```

**A card with content and actions** — compose `CardContent` and `CardActions`:
```tsx
import { Card, CardContent, CardActions, Text, Button } from "@telicent-oss/ds";

<Card maxWidth={400}>
  <CardContent>
    <Text variant="h3">Dataset</Text>
    <Text>1,240 records</Text>
  </CardContent>
  <CardActions>
    <Button variant="secondary">Cancel</Button>
    <Button variant="primary">Open</Button>
  </CardActions>
</Card>
```

---
**Agent Rule Checklist Before Generating JSX:**
1. Did I import `style.css` + `fontawesome.css` and wrap the app in `UIThemeProvider`?
2. Did I use `Card` instead of a custom styled `div`?
3. Are all texts wrapped in `H1`–`H6` or `Text`?
4. Did I use `Button` with the right `variant` instead of styling a raw `<button>`?
5. Are loading states handled by `Skeleton`, `Spinner`, or a `disabled` button?
6. Did I set colours via the theme palette (`useExtendedTheme`) rather than Tailwind colour classes?

---

## Package and install

- Install with `yarn add @telicent-oss/ds`
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@telicent-oss/ds
- Live examples (Storybook): https://telicent-oss.github.io/telicent-ds/
- Source and issues: https://github.com/telicent-oss/telicent-ds

This reference documents @telicent-oss/ds v3.0.0.
